race.name: Granfondo Novi Sad
race.tagline: UCI Gran Fondo World Series through Fruška Gora National Park

climate.overview: Early June: typically 15-25°C (59-77°F), mild but variable

terrain.overview: Road cycling through Fruška Gora National Park: two major climbs, rolling ridge, descents, flat sprint finish

logistics.explanation: The event offers clear start procedures with packet pickup available the day before and morning of at a central Novi Sad location, plus well-marked feed and technical zones at Crveni Cot and Iriski Venac; however, roads are only partially closed, requiring riders to follow traffic rules, docking one point[1][2].

length.explanation: Distances of 77km and 100km provide solid gran fondo options suitable for various abilities, but fall short of ultra-long epic routes over 120km that define top-tier endurance tests[1].

technicality.explanation: The route features challenging descent hazards like sharp left turns at 32km and 33.5km, a full-road water drain at 34km, and a dangerous S-bend into Vrdnik at 38.5km, demanding solid bike-handling skills without extreme complexity[2].

elevation.explanation: Multiple climbs including the second ascent to Iriski Venac at 61.4km where routes split offer meaningful but moderate vertical challenge through Vojvodina's landscapes, not rivaling alpine gran fondos[1][2].

climate.explanation: June timing in Novi Sad delivers favorable early summer weather with lush nature and comfortable temperatures ideal for road cycling in Serbia's South Bačka region[1][3].

altitude.explanation: Novi Sad's low-lying Danube plain location around 80m above sea level provides flat-to-rolling terrain with negligible high-altitude effects, limiting the score for elevation enthusiasts[1][3].

adventure.explanation: Riding through Vojvodina's blend of lush nature, urban Novi Sad landscapes, and regional beauty like post-race explorations creates an engaging discovery of Serbia's hidden treasures[1][3].

prestige.explanation: As a UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier, it holds solid European standing in its second edition, attracting international cyclists but not yet matching the sport's most iconic events[3][6].

race_quality.explanation: UCI-sanctioned organization with timed starts from a flag drop, age-category winners, and safety warnings at key points ensures a professional, high-quality competitive experience[2][6].

experience.explanation: The unforgettable mix of scenic Vojvodina routes, cultural Novi Sad heritage, and post-race city exploration delivers a top-tier immersive cycling adventure for all levels[1][3].

community.explanation: While fostering a global cyclist gathering as a World Series event, the emerging second edition in Novi Sad lacks the massive, deeply entrenched local cycling scene of longer-established gran fondos[3].

field_depth.explanation: UCI status draws diverse international and regional competitors across age groups, but as a newer event, it doesn't yet feature the elite depth of mature World Series qualifiers[3][6].

value.explanation: Exceptional bang-for-buck with UCI prestige, scenic routes, and Novi Sad access at accessible entry fees makes it a standout affordable gran fondo option[1][3].

expenses.explanation: Central European location in Novi Sad keeps travel and accommodation costs reasonable for most Europeans, enhanced by local tourist infrastructure without excessive add-ons[1][3].

biased_opinion.overview: Scenic UCI qualifier in wine country with perfect entry-level climbs and massive value. Fruška Gora's monasteries and vineyards make it unforgettable.

logistics.overview: Start/finish: Provincial Government building, Novi Sad city center. Nearest airport: Belgrade Nikola Tesla (BEG, ~90km). Organized by VABK.

history.overview: First UCI Gran Fondo World Series event in 2024. Second edition 2025, third on June 7-8, 2026. Novi Sad: 2022 European Capital of Culture.

course_description.character: Circular route: neutral start via Rainbow Bridge along Danube to Beočin. **Suffering Zone 1**: 19km - 9km climb (4.5% avg, 8% max). Descent through Mala Remeta/Jazak to Vrdnik. **Suffering Zone 2**: 55km - 6km climb (3.8% avg, 7% max). 11km rolling Fruška Gora ridge (selective). Return descent. Medio splits at 62km. 2km flat sprint.

non_negotiables: UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier
Fruška Gora National Park vineyards & monasteries

research_metadata.data_completeness: High for route/elevation, medium for logistics/field

videos.title: Gran Fondo Novi Sad 2025
videos.description:
08.06.2025, full footage, no edit.
Enable subtitles to see speed/power/heart rate and other data for first 55minutes
(special thanks to  @SergeyRagozin  )

Road race 100km, 1500m elevation.
https://www.strava.com/activities/14732654480

00:00 Start and 20km flats.. Find your butt, dear participant! I bet I recorded almost everyone )
22:15 Beocin Kruzni put
27:50 the first big uphill 'Crveni Cot'
50:40 downhill from Crveni Cot
55:40 some false flat parts somewhere between uphills
56:45 downhill to Beocin, with 2 other guys
1:02:55 some 10k before finish, in a group of 3, 2 minutes behind leaders
1:06:05 last 500 meters, finish
videos.curation_reason: Most recent full race coverage with detailed course breakdown including specific climbs and elevation profile.

videos.title: Gran Fondo Novi Sad Serbia 2025 - shorter route 77km
videos.description:
A chronological mix of short clips from the Gran Fondo race in Novi Sad, Serbia, in June 2025.
This shorter route is actually called Medio Fondo and it is 77km long, starting and ending in Novi Sad, riding on the roads of the nearby mountain Fruska Gora
